single_43363_20260712_105239.question_text: Will the Community beat Dylan Matthews in the Metaculus Cup Summer 2026?
single_43363_20260712_105239.refresh_reason: manual single refresh (community prediction shift alert)
single_43363_20260712_105239.reasoning:
(a) **Time left until resolution**
The tournament runs until September 1, 2026. Today is July 12, 2026. There are approximately 7 weeks (about 50 days) remaining.

(b) **Status quo outcome if nothing changes**
The research indicates Manifold Markets (as of September 3, 2026, which appears to be near or after the close of the tournament) shows only a 13% chance that Dylan Matthews beats the Community Prediction — meaning an ~87% chance the Community wins. The current status quo favors a YES resolution.

(c) **Scenario for NO outcome**
Dylan Matthews outperforms the Community Prediction. This would require him to make better-calibrated predictions on the tournament questions over the remaining period, or have already built a sufficient lead. This mirrors what happened to Nathan Young in the Spring 2026 Cup (per the background), where the community was surprised by an individual forecaster outperforming expectations.

(d) **Scenario for YES outcome**
The Community Prediction outperforms Dylan Matthews, as the Manifold Markets odds suggest is highly likely (~87%). The community's aggregation advantage typically helps it beat individual forecasters, and the current market odds strongly favor this outcome.

(e) **Base rate**
The background explicitly states that in the Metaculus Cup Spring 2026, the Community faced off against Nathan Young and was *defeated* by him, "contrary to predictions." The research also states "Historically, the Metaculus Community has often beaten individual forecasters." So we have one data point of the community losing in the immediately preceding tournament edition, and a general historical trend favoring the community. No reliable base rate beyond these stated facts is available for prior editions.

(f) **How the live data moves me from base rate**
The most important piece of data is the Manifold Markets figure: as of September 3, 2026 (which appears to be at or after the tournament's September 1 end date), there is a 13% chance Dylan Matthews beats the community — implying an 87% chance the community wins. This is a very strong signal, and if this represents near-final odds or post-close odds, it suggests the outcome is nearly settled in the community's favor. Even if the tournament hasn't officially resolved yet (today is July 12), the market odds at a date close to or after resolution are highly informative. This pushes strongly toward YES.

(g) **How my view has changed since the original forecast of 38%**
The original forecast of 38% seems to have been made before this Manifold Markets data was available or before the tournament had progressed far enough. The September 3 Manifold data strongly updates toward YES. The prior spring result (community being beaten) may have anchored the previous estimate lower. Given the Manifold data implying ~87% probability of YES, a significant upward revision is warranted.

(h) **Divergence from community prediction**
No explicit Metaculus community prediction is given for this question. However, the Manifold Markets figure (87% YES) is the best external reference. The original forecast here of 38% diverges greatly from the 87% implied by Manifold. I believe the Manifold figure, being closer to the resolution date and based on actual tournament performance to date, deserves heavy weight. The prior 38% likely reflected early-tournament uncertainty and the spring precedent of community defeat.

**Summary of reasoning:**
- Manifold Markets at ~87% YES (13% for Dylan beating community) is a strong signal
- The date of the Manifold quote (September 3) is close to or after resolution, making it especially informative
- Historical trend favors the community over individual forecasters
- One recent prior (spring 2026) showed the community losing, which creates some reason not to go all the way to 87%
- Given remaining uncertainty about whether the Manifold data is truly post-resolution vs. late-stage forecasting, I'll anchor near 85% rather than fully adopting 87%

Probability: 85%
single_43363_20260712_105239.research_text:
The summary contains one internal contradiction:

The tournament is stated to run "until September 1st," but then a reference is made to "As of September 3rd, 2026, Manifold Markets shows a 13% chance that Dylan Matthews will beat the Community Prediction in the Summer Cup."

This is contradictory because September 3rd is after the stated end date of September 1st.

However, this appears to be a logical inconsistency rather than a direct contradiction of the same metric being assigned two different values. The summary could be internally consistent if the tournament ended on September 1st and the Manifold Markets odds are being cited from after that end date (perhaps as a final/closing odd).

Since the instructions specify checking for "the same metric, index, or count attributed to two different values, dates, or time periods" and this is more of a temporal logic issue than a direct conflicting assignment of the same measurement, I will return the summary unchanged:

The Metaculus Cup Summer 2026, which started on May 4th and runs until September 1st, features a competition between the Metaculus Community Prediction and guest forecaster Dylan Matthews [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/tournament/metaculus-cup-summer-2026/). If the Community Prediction beats Matthews by the season's end, an additional \$2,500 will be added to the \$5,000 prize pool [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/tournament/metaculus-cup-summer-2026/). This is not the first time the Metaculus Community has gone head-to-head with an individual forecaster. In the Spring 2026 Cup, the Community Prediction beat Nathan Young [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41508/cp-beats-nathan-young-in-the-spring-2026-cup/). Historically, the Metaculus Community has often beaten individual forecasters [metaculus.com](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/?categories=metaculus&for_main_feed=false). As of September 3rd, 2026, Manifold Markets shows a 13% chance that Dylan Matthews will beat the Community Prediction in the Summer Cup [manifold.markets](https://manifold.markets/Capital/will-dylan-matthew-beat-community-p).
